page_content:
Program Learning Outcomes Students completing a Public Policy major will:

1 Must be completed by the end of two semesters into the major 2 Must be completed by the end of four semesters into the major 3 Taken after 60 credits 4 Taken after 90 credits

1. Understand public policy problems, concepts, processes, institutions and actors through a multidisciplinary lens, with perspectives from the local to the global.

GRADUATION PLANS Click here (https://spp.umd.edu/spp_4-year_plan/) for roadmaps for graduation plans in the School of Public Policy.

2. Analyze public issues from a range of ideological, demographic,

and socioeconomic perspectives, including those that are underrepresented in the public policy process.

Additional information on developing a graduation plan can be found on the following pages:

3. Apply quantitative and qualitative tools to real-world policy issues.

4. Communicate complex ideas and ﬁndings in written and oral forms.

http://4yearplans.umd.edu

5. Demonstrate teamwork, ethical decision-making, management and
